questions.text: Who is considered the 'Functional unit of Nature'?
questions.explanation: An ecosystem is where living organisms interact among themselves and with the surrounding physical environment.

questions.text: Which of the following is a man-made (artificial) ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Crop fields and aquaria are managed by humans and are thus artificial.

questions.text: Vertical distribution of different species occupying different levels in an ecosystem is:
questions.explanation: For example, in a forest, trees occupy top, shrubs middle, and herbs bottom strata.

questions.text: The rate of biomass production is called:
questions.explanation: Productivity is expressed in g m⁻² yr⁻¹ or kcal m⁻² yr⁻¹.

questions.text: Primary productivity depends on:
questions.explanation: It is the amount of biomass produced per unit area over time by plants.

questions.text: Net Primary Productivity (NPP) is equal to:
questions.explanation: NPP is GPP minus respiration losses (R). It is the available biomass for heterotrophs.

questions.text: The rate of formation of new organic matter by consumers is called:
questions.explanation: Secondary productivity refers to the energy stored by consumers.

questions.text: What is the annual NPP of the whole biosphere?
questions.explanation: The ocean's contribution is surprisingly low (55 billion tons) despite covering 70% of Earth.

questions.text: Detritus refers to:
questions.explanation: Dead leaves, bark, flowers, and fecal matter constitute detritus.

questions.text: Which step of decomposition involves breaking detritus into smaller particles by earthworms?
questions.explanation: Earthworms are called 'friends of farmers' because they perform fragmentation.

questions.text: Humus is highly resistant to microbial action and undergoes decomposition at an:
questions.explanation: Humus is a dark, amorphous substance that serves as a nutrient reservoir.

questions.text: The process where water-soluble inorganic nutrients go down into the soil is:
questions.explanation: Nutrients get precipitated as unavailable salts deep in the soil.

questions.text: Decomposition rate is slower if detritus is rich in:
questions.explanation: Lignin and chitin are complex substances difficult to break down.

questions.text: What percentage of incident solar radiation is PAR (Photosynthetically Active Radiation)?
questions.explanation: Of the incident solar radiation, less than 50% is PAR.

questions.text: Plants capture only ________ of the PAR.
questions.explanation: This small amount of energy sustains the entire living world.

questions.text: Energy flow in an ecosystem is always:
questions.explanation: Energy flows from producers to consumers and never back to the sun.

questions.text: Lindeman's 10 percent law states that:
options: 10% energy is transferred to next level
questions.explanation: Only 10% of energy at one trophic level is available to the next higher level.

questions.text: In an aquatic ecosystem, the major conduit for energy flow is:
questions.explanation: In water bodies, GFC is more significant than DFC.

questions.text: The mass of living material at a particular time at a trophic level is:
questions.explanation: Standing crop is measured as the mass of living organisms (biomass) or the number in a unit area.

questions.text: The pyramid of biomass in the sea is generally:
questions.explanation: Because the biomass of fish exceeds that of phytoplankton.

questions.text: Which pyramid is ALWAYS upright?
questions.explanation: Energy can never be created or recycled as it moves up; it is always lost as heat.

questions.text: Primary succession occurs on:
questions.explanation: Primary succession starts where no living organisms ever existed.

questions.text: The final stable community in ecological succession is called:
questions.explanation: The climax community is in equilibrium with the environment.

questions.text: In Hydrarch succession, the climax community is a:
questions.explanation: Both hydrarch and xerarch successions lead eventually to a mesic (forest) condition.

questions.text: The pioneer species on a bare rock are usually:
questions.explanation: Lichens secrete acids to dissolve rock and help in soil formation.

questions.text: Standing state refers to the amount of ________ in the soil at any time.
questions.explanation: Standing state deals with non-living nutrients, while standing crop deals with living matter.

questions.text: The reservoir for the gaseous type of nutrient cycle is:
questions.explanation: Carbon and Nitrogen cycles have reservoirs in the atmosphere or hydrosphere.

questions.text: Phosphorus cycle is an example of:
questions.explanation: Its reservoir is located in the Earth's crust (rocks).

questions.text: What percentage of an organism's dry weight is Carbon?
questions.explanation: Carbon is next only to water in concentration within organisms.

questions.text: What percentage of global carbon is dissolved in oceans?
questions.explanation: Oceanic carbon is the major regulator of CO₂ in the atmosphere.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a functional component of an ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Stratification is a structural component, not a functional one.

questions.text: The ecological pyramid of numbers for a single big tree supporting many birds is:
questions.explanation: One tree (base) supports many birds (higher level).

questions.text: Saprophytes are placed in which trophic level in the food chain?
questions.explanation: Despite their vital role, decomposers are not given a place in traditional ecological pyramids.

questions.text: Secondary succession is ________ than primary succession.
questions.explanation: Because some soil or sediment is already present.

questions.text: What is the pioneer species in Hydrarch succession?
questions.explanation: The successional series in water starts with microscopic phytoplankton.

questions.text: The products of ecosystem processes are called:
questions.explanation: Examples include healthy soil, pure water, and pollination.

questions.text: Who gave a price tag to ecosystem services?
questions.explanation: He estimated the value of nature's services at US $33 trillion a year.

questions.text: Which of the following contributes to carbon returning to the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Burning of fossil fuels and respiration release CO₂.

questions.text: Major reservoir of Phosphorus is:
questions.explanation: Weathering of rocks releases phosphates into the soil solution.

questions.text: The number of trophic levels in a food chain is restricted because:
questions.explanation: Only 10% energy moves up, so after 4-5 levels, there isn't enough energy to support another level.
